title: Finish Memory first

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Memory is completed before Workspace or Planning. Tier 3A converts read-only report JSON and non-verbose paths to portable primitives where practical, including #1008 for memory.status text and declarative payload status IR; Tier 3B handles lifecycle commands through managed-payload and mutation-planning primitives where practical, leaving apply and mutation safety as explicit domain runtime until modeled.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: memory.status and memory.doctor non-verbose JSON/text use generated payload.status; default memory install/init/adopt dry-runs use generated payload.lifecycle-plan; memory current show JSON/text uses generated payload.current-memory plus output.emit.current-memory; memory report and route-report non-verbose text/JSON use portable compact payloads. Package runtime no longer owns those deterministic report/planning/view routes.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Remaining Memory lifecycle commands, customized lifecycle policy, current check, verbose/strict doctor detail, verbose route-report fixture evaluation, and mutation/apply paths are still part of this active lane.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Full memory suite caught a target-root resolution gap in the earlier memory.status text path: payload.status used the process cwd when text output skipped resolve_target_root. Fixed directly by running target resolution for all non-verbose status output.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: focused status/doctor/install/init/adopt/current-show JSON/text proof passed; full lane proof remains pending
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: make lint-memory, docker conformance, and remaining lane-specific checks before closing Tier 3
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: evaluate the next Memory runtime-owned path after route-report text, likely upgrade or migrate-layout dry-run only if the dry-run plan can stay declarative; do not flatten replacement/removal policy into copy-only payload planning.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented nine Tier 3 Memory slices: memory.status and memory.doctor non-verbose JSON/text now flow through declarative payload.status plus generated install-result output; default install/init/adopt dry-run planning now flows through declarative payload.lifecycle-plan; memory current show JSON/text now flows through declarative payload.current-memory plus output.emit.current-memory; memory verify-payload text/JSON now flows through payload.verify plus output.emit.install-result; memory report and route-report non-verbose text/JSON now flow through portable target-file primitives and generated text emission while verbose diagnostics remain explicit package runtime.
execution_run.scope touched: memory report/current/lifecycle/verify-payload/route-report operation contracts, payload verification policy/schema, primitive registry/executor/generator, generated memory operation/package artifacts, generated runtime facades, inventory/IR metadata, generated Python proof Dockerfiles, proof checker, focused tests, and this planning surface

execution_run.next step: Continue with the next Memory runtime-owned path; do not promote Workspace or Planning until Tier 3 Memory closeout proof is clean.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest packages/memory/tests/test_report.py::test_memory_report_tiny_does_not_build_full_report packages/memory/tests/test_report.py::test_memory_report_text_uses_compact_declarative_payload -q; uv run pytest packages/memory/tests/test_report.py::test_memory_route_report_tiny_does_not_evaluate_fixtures packages/memory/tests/test_report.py::test_memory_route_report_text_uses_compact_declarative_payload -q; uv run pytest packages/memory/tests/test_install.py::test_generated_verify_payload_text_uses_declarative_payload_policy tests/test_command_generation_primitive_executor.py::test_output_emit_serializes_module_result_objects -q; make test-memory; make lint-memory; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --primitive-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-completion-blockers --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json

execution_summary.outcome delivered: memory.status/doctor payload-status slices, default install/init/adopt dry-run payload lifecycle-plan slices, current show payload-current-memory slice, verify-payload payload.verify slice, route-report non-verbose text/JSON portable primitive slice, and memory report non-verbose text/JSON portable primitive slice implemented
execution_summary.validation confirmed: focused memory tests, full memory/workspace tests and lint, contract tooling, structured inventory, generated package checks, Node conformance, generated Python conformance, generated Python Docker conformance, primitive Docker conformance, TypeScript Docker package proof, root CLI authority defaults, and completion-blocker report
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: execplan updated with concrete scope, proof, and continuation

durable_residue.learned constraint: Generated Docker conformance proof can fail for proof-environment reasons unrelated to adapter contract drift: stale Debian bytecode in the TypeScript proof image, Python 3.13 pathlib/jsonschema crashes in the Python proof image, git-present/non-checkout assumptions, and occasional recovered signal-11 adapter subprocess crashes.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Keep proof-environment failures separate from adapter contract drift so agents do not overfit implementation changes to broken proof setup; avoid redundant heavyweight schema validation inside conformance loops when static proof already owns schema validity; keep Docker proof images on the stable Python 3.12 base until Python 3.13 container crashes are understood.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: promote if generated Python Docker conformance continues to show recovered signal-11 retries after this lane or if the retry stops being sufficient

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The memory.status and memory.doctor non-verbose routes plus default install/init/adopt dry-run routes are generated and proven, but Tier 3 Memory still includes additional lifecycle/mutation paths.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Focused tests and generated package proof passed; completion-blocker output still names remaining source-runtime ownership.
closure_check.reopen trigger: If memory.status or memory.doctor text/JSON calls package-owned status or doctor collection for non-verbose non-strict output again.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.

signals fixed.signal: Full memory proof caught that the generated memory.status text path skipped target-root resolution while payload.status still needed target_root, making the command depend on process cwd.
signals fixed.fix: memory.status now resolves target_root for all non-verbose output before payload.status runs.

signals fixed.signal: The TypeScript Docker conformance proof was less smooth than local conformance: the original image hit a Debian pip bytecode failure before conformance could run.
signals fixed.fix: Fixed directly by deleting stale Debian Python bytecode in generated/typescript/Dockerfile.conformance before invoking pip; no issue filed per instruction.

signals fixed.signal: Python Docker conformance first failed in a pathlib relative_to compatibility path during vocabulary scanning, then segfaulted during redundant jsonschema validation while building conformance cases.
signals fixed.fix: Replaced the checker's Docker fallback path normalization with os.path.relpath and made the conformance case loader read command_package_ir.json without re-running schema validation already covered by static proof.

signals fixed.signal: Python Docker conformance became noisy and unreliable on Python 3.13 images, and non-slim Python also exposed git-present/no-.git fallback assumptions.
signals fixed.fix: Moved generated Python conformance Dockerfiles to Python 3.12 and made compatibility vocabulary scanning fall back to filesystem traversal when git ls-files cannot run in the copied proof tree.

signals routed.signal: Generated Python Docker conformance still reported a recovered signal-11 retry for the root defaults adapter before passing.
signals routed.route: Keep as lane residue unless it recurs or becomes unrecovered; the current proof harness surfaces the warning and completed after retry.

drift_log: 2026-05-20: Scaffolded by agentic-planning promote-to-plan from decomposition lane tier-3-memory-first.
2026-05-20: Filled concrete scope/proof for first #1008 memory.status payload-status slice and kept Tier 3 lane open.
2026-05-20: Added memory verify-payload text/JSON payload.verify slice and fixed TypeScript Docker conformance image bytecode/pip fragility; full selected proof passed.
